segments.text: This is not a crab. In fact, this
impostor crab shares less DNA with real
crabs than a hippo does with a whale.
This is also not a crab. This is a
nightmare, but also not a crab. But if
not crab, why crabshaped? It turns out
that things just keep evolving into
crabs. And an actual leading scientific
theory is that crabs are just the
pinnacle of evolution, the ideal body
type. So, are we next? Well, about 250
million years ago, this group of
shrimplike animals split. These guys
became crabs. Four sets of legs, two
pinchers, and a flat armored body. But
this other group kept that shrimp-like
appearance with the longer tail, and
they evolved separately for millions of
years. Normally, evolution that long
leads to all kinds of amazing diversity.
Not what happened. On four separate
occasions, this group tried to become
crabs. But there are some key physical
differences. False crabs tend to only
have three sets of legs, and sometimes
they stop like halfway through
crabification. Unfortunately, you and I
do not genetically have what it takes to
become crab. But if in your heart you
are also crab,
>> subscribe.
